title: March 2022 — crisis-hotline numbers saved (1-800-273-8255 + 741741)
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line March 2022 note: just two numbers, the (now-retired) National Suicide Prevention Lifeline (1-800-273-8255, the Logic song "1-800-273-8255") and the Crisis Text Line (741741, with the trigger word "home").
  • Saved as a personal-reference card — Yahir putting mental health resources within reach during sophomore year.
  • Sits inside the same week as 2022 02 23 nursery homes's "died in smash on our final life" line and a few months before 2022 05 02 dizzy financial aid — a 2022 mental health cluster across the spring semester.
  • Cross-referenced with 2022 02 26 2009 mac miller lyrics five people rooming — Yahir was deep in the mac miller suicide-themed lyric corpus that same month.
  • The number 1-800-273-8255 has since been replaced by 988 (rolled out July 2022, four months after this note); the number being saved here is the pre-988 version.

Privacy note

Both numbers are public crisis-resource numbers (not personal contacts), so reproducing them in this summary is safe. No personal phone numbers in the source.

Why it matters

This is a resource-saved-for-later note, not a crisis note — and that distinction is the data point. Yahir was prophylactically caching the numbers, likely for himself, possibly for someone in his orbit. The same impulse shows up in his 2022 09 23 buy mom a house long-horizon care planning.

The juxtaposition with the mac miller listening cluster (2022 02 26 2009 mac miller lyrics five people rooming) and the dream-of-being-shot (2022 11 05 shot dream numb) suggests sophomore year was a period of thinking about mortality and crisis without necessarily being in one.

Open questions

  • Was this for him, for maya, or for a friend? (Note doesn't say; the prophylactic-saving framing reads more "for me / for whoever needs it.")
  • Did it ever get used? (No follow-up trace.)
